title: __JXL_DEV__ global flag read from globalThis without origin guard, injectable by co-page scripts
description: The DEV flag is computed at module load via `typeof (globalThis as any).__JXL_DEV__ !== 'undefined'`. Any script running in the same browsing context (e.g. a third-party ad or analytics script on the same origin) can set `window.__JXL_DEV__ = true` before the module loads and permanently enable verbose console.warn output in production. While this does not directly leak secrets or enable privilege escalation, it can be used to force DEV-mode assertion throws (`assertInvariants`) that are suppressed in production, potentially causing unhandled rejections or unexpected pool shutdowns. The Node.js branch correctly gates on `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'`, but the browser branch is a plain global lookup with no namespacing or integrity check.

title: sourceKey parameter accepted with no length or content validation, enabling memory pressure via large key strings
description: The `sourceKey` parameter in `acquireSlot` is stored as-is as a Map key in `DedupeRegistry.keyToSession` and `sessionToKey`. There is no length cap or character validation. A caller passing a multi-megabyte string as `sourceKey` would pin that allocation in two Maps for the duration of the session plus the time until `dedupe.complete()` is called. In a scenario where jxl-session is exposed to partially-untrusted input (e.g. URLs derived from user-supplied content without prior normalization), this could be used to inflate scheduler memory. The `sessionId` field has the same issue but is typically generated internally.

title: CoreBudget allows capacity=0, causing all acquire() calls to throw unconditionally
description: The constructor validates `capacity >= 0` (not `> 0`), so `new CoreBudget(0)` is accepted. Any subsequent `acquire(cost)` where `cost > 0` will hit the `cost > this.capacity` guard and throw `[jxl-scheduler] CoreBudget: cost N exceeds capacity 0`. This means a misconfigured or maliciously-crafted `coreBudget` option can permanently disable worker spawning across all schedulers sharing that budget instance without any warning at construction time. The `globalCoreBudget` is safe (uses `Math.max(1, ...)`), but library consumers passing a custom `CoreBudget` with capacity derived from unreliable input (e.g. `navigator.hardwareConcurrency` on unsupported browsers returning 0) will get a broken pool with no diagnostic.

title: No schema validation on acquireSlot parameters at the public API boundary
description: The `acquireSlot` method is a public API surface that accepts caller-supplied `sessionId`, `sourceKey`, `priority`, and `startMsg` without any type-narrowing validation at runtime. TypeScript types are erased at runtime, so a JavaScript caller (or a TypeScript caller with an `as any` cast) can pass unexpected values. For example, a non-string `priority` would pass through to `PriorityQueue.lane()` and hit the switch's implicit undefined return, causing a silent push to `undefined` (TypeError). Adding a lightweight guard (e.g. asserting `priority` is one of the three known strings) would harden the boundary against integration bugs and malformed protocol messages.
